summary: Iteration 2 retest after MailReply→Reply icon fix. All blocking bugs from iteration 1 are resolved. Result screen renders successfully (no 'Element type is invalid' crash) and shows every required card. End-to-end flows verified: result render, clipboard copy, history list/delete, onboarding → language → home, and backend DELETE.

react_native_issues.component: app/result.tsx onDelete (Alert.alert) and similar Alerts in history.tsx/settings.tsx
react_native_issues.issue: On the Expo web preview, react-native-web's Alert.alert does NOT render the multi-button confirm dialog (no native window.confirm fired and no in-DOM modal observed). Tapping result-delete therefore appears to do nothing on web. This is a known react-native-web limitation and would work correctly on real iOS, but it makes the destructive-confirmation UX unverifiable in the web preview. Backend DELETE /api/analyses/{id} is verified working independently (200 → 404 round-trip). Recommend verifying on iOS simulator OR swapping Alert.alert for a custom cross-platform confirm modal so the flow is testable on web.

action_items: (Optional) Replace Alert.alert in result.tsx/history.tsx with a custom RN modal (e.g., a Pressable overlay + two buttons) so destructive confirm flows work on web preview as well as native; otherwise document that delete confirmation is iOS-only and skip testing on web.

(Nice-to-have) Resolve Expo SDK package version warnings flagged in iteration 1 (async-storage, expo-camera, expo-clipboard, expo-document-picker, expo-file-system, expo-image-picker, react-native-svg) before native build.
critical_code_review_comments: result.tsx now imports `Reply` from lucide-react-native (line 27) and uses <Reply /> in the reply-card SectionRow — verified rendering correctly. Icon fix is complete.

Alert.alert is used for destructive flows in result.tsx and history.tsx; this is fine on iOS but silently no-ops on web (react-native-web limitation). Consider a portable confirm component for parity.
Clipboard integration via expo-clipboard works on web (verified clipboard.readText returns full 523-char German reply draft).
Backend continues to be solid: PoST /api/analyze JSON+base64 flow re-tested live, returns full structured AnalysisRecord and excludes Mongo _id.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the requested retest scope; result-delete confirm Alert is the only flow not verifiable on web

seed_data_creation: Main seed device_id=92f12911-efce-4b72-b0ae-a2b5d8a39af7, analysis_id=b0da98a1-d461-4762-9726-3525568f6a0a (zh, AOK Mahnung, risk red) is INTACT. A disposable second seed (id=99f5aa3c-0259-4171-aef1-aff6c520e74f, Stadtwerke Berlin Rechnung) was created and then deleted via DELETE /api/analyses/{id} during testing — backend DELETE verified (200 → subsequent GET = 404).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 2 confirms the icon fix works. All cards on the result screen render in the user's chosen language (verified in zh). Clipboard copy verified. Onboarding/language/home flow verified. The only outstanding observation is that Alert.alert confirmation dialogs are not rendered on the react-native-web preview — they will work on real iOS. If you need to test the destructive flow on web, ask the main agent to swap to a custom modal.
rca_of_issue: Iteration 1's CRITICAL bug (MailReply undefined) is resolved by the import change to `Reply` in /app/frontend/app/result.tsx (lines 27 and 295). Live verification: navigated to /result?id=b0da98a1-d461-4762-9726-3525568f6a0a with localStorage seed (deviceId=92f12911-…, language=zh, onboarded=1). The screen rendered with 0 page errors and all expected testIDs present: risk-card-red, summary-card, explanation-card, actions-card, deadlines-card, sender-card, reply-card, reply-copy, questions-card, uncertainties-card, disclaimer-card, result-analyze-again, result-delete, result-back. Clipboard test: tapping reply-copy populated navigator.clipboard with the full 523-character German reply draft and toggled the UI to '已复制' (Copied). History list shows the seeded analysis with a working Trash2 (history-delete-{id}) button. Onboarding (3 slides) → Language picker → Home reached cleanly with the UI fully localized to 简体中文.
